channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Tehran's Hormuz threat risks global fallout, Rubio warns as U.S. strikes Iran for 11th straight night
item.description: His comments come as U.S. Central Command hit Iranian targets for an 11th straight night.

item.title: Trump's push for American-made AI chips hits TSMC's margins
item.description: The Taiwan-based chipmaker has announced $200 billion in investment into U.S. manufacturing since Trump returned to power in 2025.

item.title: Wistron shares surge in Taiwan after opening of Texas AI superchip plant to supply Nvidia
item.description: Taiwan’s Wistron Corporation opens first U.S. facility in Fort Worth, Texas.

item.title: Trump plans generic drug tariffs from 2028 with two-year delay testing U.S. onshoring push
item.description: The latest tariff salvo underscored Trump's goal of reshoring low-cost drug production, but displacing established suppliers like India won't be easy.

item.title: The world's top fintech companies: 2026
item.description: Fintech is entering an era of scale and maturity, just as AI forces innovation. CNBC and Statista name the key players shaping the industry — and shaking it up.

item.title: Nike to cut off thousands of online distributors in China, restructure digital footprint
item.description: Nike is streamlining its online business in China as the company looks to stabilize pricing and branding and get back to growth.

item.title: Super Micro surges 15% on new order and margin disclosure after SpaceX announcement
item.description: Many server makers are seeing faster growth as companies race to deploy artificial intelligence servers.

item.title: Wall Street can wait: Why one U.S. biotech firm is listing in Hong Kong first
item.description: Global biotech firms are increasingly being drawn to Hong Kong for its growing investor base and proximity to Chinese pharmaceutical partners.

item.title: Goldman Sachs creates private markets platform as rich investors seek the next SpaceX and Stripe
item.description: Goldman Sachs is launching a new alternative investments platform aimed at giving wealthy clients and family offices direct stakes in private companies.

item.title: OpenAI appoints two new members to board of directors
item.description: OpenAI appointed longtime financial executives David Vélez and Robin Vince to its nonprofit and for-profit boards.

item.title: These Japanese AI winners don’t make chips. They make toilets, glass fiber and seasoning
item.description: A toilet maker, a glass fiber manufacturer and the maker of MSG have in Japan have become major beneficiaries of the AI boom.

item.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
item.description: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

item.title: Sony’s 'ironic' PlayStation disc decision upends gamer conventions and threatens a $7 billion resale market
item.description: In 2013, Sony tweaked Xbox by showing how easy it was to share a PlayStation disc. Now, gamers say Sony has become exactly what it mocked.

item.title: The Fed rang the alarm about Anthropic's Mythos AI model — but had to go months without it
item.description: The central bank didn't have access to Claude Mythos Preview as of mid-July, even as other institutions were racing to patch their vulnerabilities.

item.title: Capital One earnings were good enough but didn't answer the big question hanging over the stock
item.description: Investors want to know when Capital One will begin to see more tangible benefits from its Discover and Brex deals.

item.title: The sell-off in Korean stocks may soon subside. That could be good news for U.S. markets
item.description: South Korea's stock market could be turning around — and some investors expect its U.S. counterpart won't be far behind.

item.title: Apple prepares a new way to buy iPhones. Plus, making sense of Google's new AI models
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Japan exports and imports in June grow at fastest pace since November 2022, beating estimates
item.description: Exports climbed 19.3%, while imports spiked 25.4% compared to June last year.

item.title: India steps up energy shift as costs and tariff pressures mount — oil blending push sparks backlash
item.description: India is banking on alternative fuels as it prioritizes energy security amid high oil prices and supply constraints.

item.title: Ready for takeoff: This stock’s next growth runway is securing identity in an AI age
item.description: A resilient airport franchise has become a customer acquisition engine powering this stock's broader identity platform and giving it a competitive advantage.

item.title: The FDA made a big reversal in its cyclosporiasis probe, but it still thinks iceberg lettuce is to blame
item.description: The FDA's investigation into the source of the cyclosporiasis outbreak is causing confusion as companies and consumers alike navigate new updates.

item.title: Have less-than-perfect credit? These 4 credit cards help you upgrade from a secured card
item.description: Secured cards are often used to help build or re-establish credit, but they can also provide a direct pipeline to an unsecured card.

item.title: GM announces new gas-powered Cadillac vehicles amid EV pullback
item.description: GM CEO Mary Barra said Tuesday the next-generation Cadillacs will include new versions of the CT5 sedan, XT5 midsize SUV and discontinued three-row XT6 SUV.

item.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano dismisses JPMorgan spying allegations
item.description: Bisignano denied allegations The Wall Street Journal reported that he surveilled his colleagues while working at JPMorgan more than a decade ago.

item.title: Prediction markets and their opponents are spending more to influence Congress in 2026
item.description: Kalshi and groups representing the casino and gaming industry and online sports betting apps, all have boosted their lobbying spending in 2026.

item.title: RFK Jr. says cyclospora outbreak is 'under control'
item.description: Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday said that the ongoing outbreak of cyclosporiasis is "under control."

item.title: Mamdani says New York lacks authority to arrest Netanyahu, calls him ‘war criminal’
item.description: Israel's prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, is the target of an arrest warrant issued by the International Criminal Court related to the war on Gaza.

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires military chief after protests and names a replacement
item.description: Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief, according to a statement on social media.

item.title: Bets against SpaceX grow to 32% of float as Elon Musk warns short sellers won't survive
item.description: About 206 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 32% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

item.title: Time for a crypto comeback? Traders bet that this stock will lead the way
item.description: Crypto bulls have been waiting for a rebound, and traders think this stock could lead the way.
